skill.name: crypto-bd-agent
skill.description: Guides AI agents for cryptocurrency business development, including token discovery, weighted scoring, wallet forensics, x402 payment planning, ERC-8004 identity, and listing outreach workflows.
skill.summary: Build crypto BD agents for token discovery, scoring, wallet review, and listing outreach.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network, credential, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. AI review found the skill is documentation-only, with fenced text diagrams, reference URLs, and safety guidance rather than executable code or secret access. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Fenced Text
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections map to Markdown fenced text diagrams and flow descriptions. No executable command, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown code fences labeled text. They describe architecture and model routing, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are GitHub reference links to an implementation. The skill does not contain code that sends requests, uploads data, or contacts those URLs automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain documentation references. No network API call or data transfer logic exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Private Key Mention Is Defensive Guidance
low_findings.description: The private key finding appears in a security rule telling users never to share API keys or wallet private keys. It does not request secrets, read secrets, or instruct exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a prohibition on sharing secrets. This is safety guidance rather than credential access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to be triggered by crypto-domain vocabulary and agent registration text. No hash algorithm, cipher selection, key derivation, or custom cryptographic implementation is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss cryptocurrency business workflows and agent metadata. No weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA1, DES, or RC4 is present.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Business Workflow Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings refer to intelligence sources, ROI tracking, deployer fund analysis, and paid API logging. They do not enumerate the host system, collect local environment data, or run discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text concerns crypto market research and audit logging. It does not contain host reconnaissance commands or local system inspection logic.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination of execution, network access, and credential access was not confirmed. The file contains documentation, reference URLs, and defensive key-handling guidance, with no runnable code path connecting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each component of the heuristic resolves to non-executable documentation. No semantic evidence shows malicious chaining or exfiltration intent.

low_findings.title: Financial Automation Requires Operational Caution
low_findings.description: The skill discusses autonomous crypto business development, paid data calls, x402 micropayments, and wallet separation. This is legitimate guidance, but users should apply human approval and verified endpoints before spending funds or contacting projects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The operational risk is explicit because the documentation covers paid API calls and wallet workflows. The risk is limited because the skill is not executable code.

content.value_statement: Crypto teams need structured ways to find, score, and qualify token listing prospects. This skill provides repeatable agent patterns for discovery, wallet review, outreach, and human-approved pipeline management.
actual_capabilities: Defines a 100-point framework for scoring token listing prospects.
Outlines multi-source token discovery across DEX, social, safety, and wallet data.
Describes wallet forensics checks for deployer funding, holdings, transfers, and identity signals.
Maps a 10-stage exchange listing pipeline from discovery to listing.
Explains LLM cascade routing for routine, analysis, and strategy tasks.
Documents security rules for private keys, paid endpoints, and human approval.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not a runnable scanner or trading system.
It does not provide API credentials, wallet software, or exchange integrations.
Users must verify token data, legal requirements, and outreach claims independently.
It does not replace financial, legal, compliance, or security review.

use_cases.title: Token Prospect Research
use_cases.description: Build a repeatable workflow for finding and ranking new token listing candidates.

use_cases.title: Wallet Risk Review
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for deployer wallet history, funding sources, balances, and risk flags.

use_cases.title: Agent Workflow Design
use_cases.description: Plan an AI-assisted pipeline with model routing, paid data controls, and human approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Scoring Rubric
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a simple token scoring rubric for exchange listing research. Include liquidity, market cap, volume, social activity, age, and team transparency.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Token Prospect
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate this token prospect. Summarize source confirmations, score each factor, list wallet forensics checks, and recommend next pipeline stage.

prompt_templates.title: Design a BD Agent Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow planning for an agent team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an AI crypto BD pipeline. Include data sources, scoring thresholds, LLM routing, human approvals, paid API controls, and outreach stages.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Autonomous Payment Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit an x402-enabled crypto BD workflow. Identify endpoint verification steps, wallet separation, spending limits, logging needs, and human approval gates.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether a token with strong liquidity but anonymous founders should enter outreach.
output_examples.output: A scored recommendation that highlights liquidity strength, team transparency risk, required wallet checks, and a cautious next-stage decision.

output_examples.input: Plan a daily discovery process for Solana and Ethereum prospects.
output_examples.output: A source plan covering DEX data, safety checks, wallet review, social signals, paid-source limits, and a top-five daily shortlist.

output_examples.input: Prepare security rules for an agent that uses paid crypto data sources.
output_examples.output: A safety checklist covering verified endpoints, separate wallets, spending logs, private key handling, and human approval before outreach.

best_practices: Require two independent source confirmations before qualifying a prospect.
Keep human approval before outreach, spending, or public agent actions.
Separate payment, posting, and routing wallets to reduce blast radius.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on token names without verified contract and pair addresses.
Do not automate outreach or payments without approval gates and logs.
Do not treat scoring outputs as financial advice or compliance approval.

faq.question: Does this skill trade cryptocurrency?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on business development workflows for token discovery, evaluation, and exchange listing outreach.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is guidance documentation and does not include scripts, commands, or API clients.

faq.question: Can it handle private keys?
faq.answer: No. It instructs users not to share API keys or wallet private keys.

faq.question: What chains does it discuss?
faq.answer: It discusses multi-chain workflows, with examples for Solana, Ethereum, Base, and other supported data sources.

faq.question: Who should review outputs before action?
faq.answer: A qualified human operator should approve outreach, paid calls, wallet actions, and listing decisions.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
